jobStrategy.readerGoal: Show end-to-end product engineering with Python/Django or FastAPI backends, React UI, APIs, databases, deployment, and product judgment.
trimPriorities: ML-only projects when product value is weak
claimRules: Do not claim TypeScript, on-call, CI/CD, GraphQL, microservices, or production....
Do not claim tools, platforms, or production scope unless the resume proves the...

whatWentWell: Extraction found React, PostgreSQL, AWS, APIs, CI/CD, GraphQL, microservices, security, deployment, and architecture.
Selection kept BitGo, Centi, and Taylor.io.
Unsupported exact terms like CI/CD, GraphQL, and microservices were not claimed directly.
whatWentWrong: Strategy picked sales_growth instead of full-stack product/platform engineering.
Tailor Assist explained the draft as customer-facing growth and revenue-adjacent impact.
Skills trimming removed useful backend/database breadth like MySQL, MongoDB, and ETL Pipelines.
BitGo and Taylor.io rewrites were too light.
previousRun.biggestConcern: The system selected decent rows despite the wrong strategy, but the wrong strategy poisoned the summary, skills, and Tailor Assist explanation.

jobStrategy.readerGoal: Frame data, analytics, reporting, dashboards, and AI workflow ownership while staying cautious about lead-level or domain-specific claims.
claimRules: Do not claim healthcare data, Claude, Excel, or true lead-level ownership unles...
Unsupported exact AI, cloud, or data platforms belong in cautions, not resume c...
summaryGuardrails: Use ownership language cautiously for lead roles.
Avoid unsupported healthcare or executive authority claims.

whatWentWell: Selection was the strongest part.
The pipeline correctly surfaced BitGo, Taylor.io, Centi, and SentimentTrader as the best available evidence for data workflows, AI tools, analytics, reporting-adjacent work, and product/data systems.
It correctly avoided claiming unsupported exact tools like Claude, healthcare data, and Excel.
whatWentWrong: Strategy picked sales_growth instead of a data/AI analytics ownership stretch lane.
Tailor Assist talked about customer-facing growth and revenue-adjacent impact instead of data infrastructure, analytics, reporting, and AI operations.
Extraction caught data and AI, but missed the deeper operating model: first data hire, data repository, ROI reporting, dashboards, operations, and multi-source integrations.
Summary was directionally data/AI focused, but too confident for a Lead role.
Skills trimming removed useful bridge skills like AWS EC2 and Django.
previousRun.biggestConcern: The system found the right resume evidence, but shaped and explained it through the wrong sales/growth lane instead of a data infrastructure, analytics, reporting, and AI operations lane.

jobStrategy.readerGoal: Show direct hospitality fit through guest communication, phone etiquette, teamwork, pace, professionalism, and service reliability.
claimRules: Do not treat software projects as proof for a host or hospitality role unless e...
Do not call the candidate a sales professional unless formal sales or outreach....

whatWentWell: The pipeline correctly identified Hawkers Host / Expo and Bar Louie Server as the core evidence.
The service experience rewrites focused on communication, coordination, high-pressure hospitality, and guest experience.
The rewrite stage was the strongest part of the run.
whatWentWrong: The system treated a direct hospitality fit as a stretch.
Strategy used sales_growth instead of a hospitality/customer service lane.
Customer service, hospitality, and dining were suppressed as context even though they are central to the JD.
The pipeline kept too much unrelated technical material.
Centi was edited even though software projects should usually be dropped for this role.
Tailor Assist talked about customer-facing growth and product interest, which does not match the JD.
previousRun.biggestConcern: The system had the right service evidence, but classified the role as a stretch sales/growth case instead of a direct hospitality/customer service fit, which caused the explanation and project handling to drift.

tailorAssistShouldNotMention: software product interest as the main story

jobStrategy.readerGoal: Use engineering problem-solving, testing mindset, documentation, and cross-functional collaboration as a cautious bridge into physical product development.
claimRules: Do not claim HVAC, CAD, thermodynamics, airflow, manufacturing, or lab testing....
Do not treat weak keyword overlap as direct experience.
summaryGuardrails: Do not write a full-stack/backend/data-pipeline summary.
Make the stretch clear and honest.

whatWentWell: Extraction was solid and found HVAC, airflow, testing, compliance, product lifecycle, manufacturing, and cross-functional work.
The pipeline correctly treated HVAC, airflow, residential systems, and manufacturing as unsupported instead of pretending Colin had that background.
Keeping ShelfVision made some sense as the closest product/testing-style project.
whatWentWrong: Strategy picked sales_growth instead of an engineering product development stretch lane.
Tailor Assist said it prioritized customer-facing growth and revenue-adjacent impact, which is completely wrong for this JD.
The summary drifted into full-stack/backend/data pipeline language instead of testing, documentation, troubleshooting, and engineering process.
Bar Louie and Centi were weak fits and should not have been central.
Skills trimming removed potentially useful technical bridge evidence like TensorFlow Lite and Data Pipelines.
previousRun.biggestConcern: The system extracted the right mechanical/product engineering signals, but routed the role into a sales/growth lane and then overcorrected into generic software language instead of a cautious engineering product development stretch.

unsupportedWarnings: 5 years of Agile Business Analyst experience

jobStrategy.readerGoal: Bridge software-building evidence into requirements, process, documentation, testing, and stakeholder-facing systems analysis without claiming senior BA credentials.
keepPriorities: technical documentation or structured data projects
trimPriorities: unsupported BA certifications or enterprise tooling
claimRules: Do not claim 5-10 years of BA experience, CBAP/PMI, BPM tools, Azure DevOps, or...
Use workflow and reporting language only where the resume supports the underlyi...
summaryGuardrails: Do not call the candidate a senior certified business analyst.
Position as a technical builder with adjacent process experience.
gapSignals: 5 years of Agile Business Analyst experience

whatWentWell: The pipeline correctly treated the role as a high-risk stretch instead of pretending Colin was already a senior Functional Analyst.
Selection was directionally reasonable by keeping BitGo, Bar Louie, Centi, and Taylor.io.
The kept evidence can support stakeholder communication, workflows, product thinking, user-facing systems, and process-adjacent work.
whatWentWrong: Strategy picked executive_leadership instead of a functional analyst or business systems bridge lane.
Extraction got noisy and over-weighted weak terms like years, user, and proven.
The system missed the cleaner role shape: requirements gathering, process mapping, business systems analysis, user stories, backlog support, testing/UAT, and stakeholder documentation.
BitGo stayed too close to the original instead of being reframed around technical translation, workflows, documentation, and stakeholder-facing systems.
Summary was directionally right but too confident with phrases like proven ability and technical specifications.
Tailor Assist warned about stretch fit, but used awkward explanations like senior leadership scope and strongest direct overlap was user.
previousRun.biggestConcern: The system understood that this was a stretch, but routed it through executive leadership instead of a functional analyst bridge lane, causing the strategy, summary, and explanation to focus on the wrong kind of caution.

jobStrategy.readerGoal: Show end-to-end product engineering with Python/Django or FastAPI backends, React UI, APIs, databases, deployment, and product judgment.
trimPriorities: ML-only projects when product value is weak
claimRules: Do not claim TypeScript, on-call, CI/CD, GraphQL, microservices, or production....
Do not claim tools, platforms, or production scope unless the resume proves the...
gapSignals: well-tested code if not directly evidenced

whatWentWell: Extraction was strong and found Python, React, TypeScript, Django, full-stack engineering, customer-facing products, internal products, and product/engineering collaboration signals.
The pipeline correctly treated TypeScript as unsupported exact evidence instead of inventing it.
It correctly kept BitGo and Centi, and dropping service roles was reasonable.
whatWentWrong: Strategy picked data_ai instead of full-stack product engineering.
The role was about building full-stack products from idea to launch, but the pipeline forced a data/AI narrative.
Taylor.io should likely have been a must-keep because it supports AI-native product work, full-stack ownership, backend/frontend integration, and product iteration.
Tizirsso is more of a maybe, not a stronger hero than Taylor.io.
BitGo rewrite was too light and needed stronger end-to-end ownership, product, API, and stakeholder framing.
Summary over-focused on data pipelines instead of full-stack product ownership.
Skills trimming removed useful evidence like AWS EC2, REST API Design, and AI-Driven Workflows.
Tailor Assist said it prioritized data, AI, automation, and backend evidence, which missed the actual role story.
previousRun.biggestConcern: The system saw the right full-stack job signals but routed them into a data/AI lane, causing the summary, skills, selection priority, and Tailor Assist explanation to drift away from full-stack product ownership.

jobStrategy.readerGoal: Frame customer-facing communication, trust-building, finance interest, coachability, and service reliability for financial education or representative work.
claimRules: Do not write the summary as a full-stack engineer applying to finance.
Do not claim proven representative or advisory experience unless evidenced.
Do not call the candidate a sales professional unless formal sales or outreach....
summaryGuardrails: Sound customer-facing and finance-interested, not like a technical finance engi...

whatWentWell: The pipeline correctly avoided pretending the candidate was already a proven representative.
It kept finance-adjacent evidence like BitGo and Centi.
It trimmed unrelated technical projects like SecureScape, ShelfVision, and SentimentTrader, which makes sense for a non-technical financial services role.
whatWentWrong: The pipeline over-indexed on the word financial and under-indexed on the actual job behavior.
It missed the key signals: helping people manage money, communicating clearly, learning through training, and building trust.
It dropped Bar Louie and Hawkers, which are likely the strongest proof for people-facing communication and trust-building.
The summary framed Colin as a full-stack engineer focused on financial services, which is the wrong angle.
Tailor Assist said Bar Louie and Hawkers were weaker signals, which is exactly backwards for this role.
previousRun.biggestConcern: The system treated financial keyword overlap as stronger than role behavior. For no-experience-required financial representative roles, customer-facing service evidence should beat technical finance projects.

avoidPhrases: made OpenAI, Python, and JavaScript easy to find
Conversational AI Engineer with proven experience

tailorAssistShouldNotMention: generic data/AI as the main story
only OpenAI, Python, and JavaScript as the role story

jobStrategy.readerGoal: Show Python backend, OpenAI/LLM integration, API workflows, and cloud-adjacent deployment without claiming unsupported AI platforms.
claimRules: Do not claim Dialogflow, Google CES, Amazon Bedrock, GCP, Azure, or CI/CD unles...
Unsupported exact AI, cloud, or data platforms belong in cautions, not resume c...
summaryGuardrails: Do not write a generic AI expert summary.
Name only AI/cloud tools the resume proves.

whatWentWell: Extraction found the real role signals: OpenAI, Python, LLMs, Dialogflow, conversational AI, APIs, cloud, Git, and CI/CD.
The pipeline correctly flagged unsupported exact tools like Dialogflow, Google CES, Amazon Bedrock, Azure, and Google Cloud Platform instead of allowing direct claims.
The pipeline made the right basic selection call by dropping Bar Louie and Hawkers for this technical role.
Keeping BitGo, Taylor.io, and SentimentTrader was directionally reasonable.
whatWentWrong: Strategy picked data_ai, which was close but too broad.
The role needed a more specific AI backend / LLM integration lane.
SecureScape as a hero project was questionable.
BitGo and SentimentTrader rewrites still looked like light wording changes.
Skills pruning removed important bridge skills like AWS EC2, ETL Pipelines, Automation Systems, and REST API Design.
The summary felt generic and did not clearly tell the Python backend + LLM integration + API story.
Tailor Assist was too shallow and said it made OpenAI, Python, and JavaScript easy to find instead of explaining the actual LLM integration/backend fit.
previousRun.biggestConcern: The system understood this was an AI role, but not the more precise story of Python backend services, OpenAI/LLM integration, APIs, workflow automation, and cloud-adjacent deployment. It should preserve supported bridge skills when exact JD platforms are unsupported.
